sections.heading: Design, suitability and performance requirements for financial products and financial
sections.title: Design, suitability and performance requirements for financial products
sections.summary: This section mandates that banks design financial products and services with due regard to financial customers' interests. It requires that relevant personnel possess necessary skills and knowledge, and that banks establish ongoing oversight mechanisms to monitor product suitability and performance. The requirements apply to banks providing financial products and services to customers.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes baseline requirements for financial product design and oversight that impact cross-border data transfers involving financial products, particularly regarding customer suitability and performance metrics.

requirements.text: A bank must design its financial products and financial services with due regard to the interests of its financial customers
A bank must ensure relevant personnel responsible for design possess necessary skills, knowledge and expertise
A bank must establish and implement ongoing oversight arrangements to monitor and review financial product design and suitability

sections.source_quote: A bank must design its financial products and financial services, Including the related models utilised for the advertising, distribution and provision of these financial products and financial services, with due regard to the interests of Its financial customers.

sections.heading: Additlonal requirements in respect of retail financial CUStOMETS ........::ssccsessssereareees 10
sections.title: Additional requirements for retail financial customers
sections.summary: This section specifies additional obligations for banks providing financial products to retail customers. It requires banks to assess customer needs, ensure products remain appropriate over time, provide flexibility for changing customer needs, and ensure contractual terms are not unfair. The requirements apply specifically to retail financial customers and their contracts.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes requirements for retail financial products that impact data transfer assessments, particularly regarding customer suitability, ongoing product relevance, and contractual fairness.

requirements.text: A bank must assess customer needs and characteristics when designing financial products for retail customers
A bank must ensure financial products remain appropriate to meet target customer needs over time
A bank must ensure reasonable flexibility in financial product design to address changing customer needs
A bank must ensure contractual terms and conditions are not unfair to retail customers

sections.source_quote: A bank that provides financial products or financial services to retail financial customers must ... ensure that the terms, conditions and requirements in a contract between the bank and its retail financial customer, relating to a financial product or financial service, Including fees and charges, are not unfair.

sections.heading: Termination, closure or switching of financlal products or financlal services by the
sections.title: Refusal and termination of financial products
sections.summary: Banks must document processes for refusing or terminating financial products/services, provide reasonable notice before termination, and disclose reasons for refusal or termination. Banks may terminate without notice if legally compelled, suspected of illegal use, or required to report to authorities.
sections.dtia_summary: Refusal and termination processes impact data transfer assessments by requiring clear communication and documentation of data handling changes, which affects data retention and deletion procedures.

requirements.text: Banks must document processes for refusing or terminating financial products/services
Banks must provide reasonable prior notice before termination
Banks must disclose reasons for refusal or termination
Banks may terminate without notice if legally compelled
Banks may terminate without notice if suspected of illegal use
Banks may terminate without notice if required to report to authorities

sections.source_quote: A bank must, subject to other applicable regulatory requirements, document, adopt and implement processes and procedures relating to the refusal to provide a financial product or render a financial service to one or more financial customers.

sections.heading: Short title ANG COMMENCOMEONE.......:::ssescccccsccssssssscccnsssesseuserenecsenenscessnssasseoooserssessessensss 23
sections.title: Short title and commencement
sections.summary: This section provides the short title and commencement details for the Conduct Standard. It does not contain substantive obligations but establishes the legal framework for the standard's application.

sections.heading: Culture and governance (cont.)
sections.title: Advertising standards for financial products
sections.summary: Banks must advertise financial products to customers in a clear, fair, and non-misleading manner. This includes ensuring factual accuracy, avoiding fraudulent statements, using plain language for retail customers, and maintaining responsibility for third-party advertising activities. Banks must also correct inconsistent advertising promptly and retain records for at least five years.
sections.dtia_summary: Advertising standards require banks to ensure transparency and accuracy in communications, which impacts how data is shared with customers through marketing channels. This affects data transfer compliance as advertising practices influence customer data collection and consent mechanisms.

requirements.text: Banks must ensure advertising is clear, fair, and not misleading
Banks must ensure advertisements are factually correct
Banks must not contain fraudulent, untrue, or misleading statements in advertisements
Banks must use plain language for retail customer advertising
Banks must ensure third-party advertising complies with this standard
Banks must correct inconsistent advertising promptly
Banks must notify persons who relied on inconsistent advertising
Banks must retain advertising records for at least five years

sections.source_quote: A bank must ensure that its financial products and financial services are advertised to financial customers in a way that is clear, fair and not misleading.

sections.heading: Culture and governance (cont.)
sections.title: Complaints management framework requirements
sections.summary: Banks must establish and maintain a complaints management framework that is proportionate to their business, includes fair treatment of complainants, and covers internal escalation processes. The framework must categorize complaints, record data, and report to the governing body. Banks must also ensure transparency in complaint handling and provide clear communication to complainants.
sections.dtia_summary: Complaints management frameworks impact data transfer assessments by requiring banks to handle customer data complaints transparently and securely, which affects data sharing practices and third-party data processing.

requirements.text: Banks must establish an adequate and effective complaints management framework
Banks must regularly review complaints management frameworks
Banks must categorize reportable complaints into specific categories
Banks must record and report on complaint categories
Banks must maintain records of complaints including details, evidence, categorization, and progress
Banks must analyze complaints data to manage conduct risks
Banks must provide clear communication to complainants about complaint progress
Banks must ensure complaints processes are transparent and accessible

sections.source_quote: A bank must establish, maintain and operate an adequate and effective complaints management framework to ensure the fair treatment of complainants.

sections.title: Refusal and termination of financial products
sections.summary: Banks must document processes for refusing or terminating financial products/services, provide reasonable notice before termination, and disclose reasons for refusal or termination. Banks may terminate without notice if legally compelled, suspected of illegal use, or required to report to authorities.
sections.dtia_summary: Refusal and termination processes impact data transfer assessments by requiring clear communication and documentation of data handling changes, which affects data retention and deletion procedures.

requirements.text: Banks must document processes for refusing or terminating financial products/services
Banks must provide reasonable prior notice before termination
Banks must disclose reasons for refusal or termination
Banks may terminate without notice if legally compelled
Banks may terminate without notice if suspected of illegal use
Banks may terminate without notice if required to report to authorities

sections.source_quote: A bank must, subject to other applicable regulatory requirements, document, adopt and implement processes and procedures relating to the refusal to provide a financial product or render a financial service to one or more financial customers.
